[Baseline lift for $\Psi_i$, $\Theta$, and $V_i$]\label{thm:baseline-lift}
Assume $\alpha>3/4$. Let
\begin{equation*}
\Psi_i=I_i(\xi_i),
\qquad
\Theta=\Psi_1\Psi_2,
\qquad
V_i=I_i(\Theta).
\end{equation*}
Then the cutoff approximations converge almost surely in the path spaces
\begin{equation}\label{eq:baseline-paths}
\Psi_i\in C_T\mathcal{C}^{\alpha-3/2-},
\qquad
\Theta\in C_T\mathcal{C}^{2\alpha-3-},
\qquad
V_i\in C_T\mathcal{C}^{3\alpha-3-},
\qquad
\partial_tV_i\in C_T\mathcal{C}^{2\alpha-3-}.
\end{equation}
In particular, $\Theta$ is a cross-Wick product without subtracting a scalar constant.
